key_highlights: Fed holds rates steady under new Chair Warsh, hawkish surprise triggers selloff then rebound
US-Iran ceasefire deal signed, oil prices slide but geopolitical jitters persist with Lebanon and Hormuz
Record weekly inflows into US stocks, led by tech, despite Fed uncertainty
Gold set for weekly loss on hawkish Fed and peace deal hopes
Indian mega IPOs loom: NSE and Jio Platforms file for listing
UK bonds fall after Andy Burnham win, political risk rises
Intel leads chip sector higher on foundry optimism
AI-driven cost crisis flagged as un-priced risk for Wall Street
Gas prices fall below $4 for first time since March
Bitcoin options traders bet on drop to $52,000

sectors.trend: Strong rebound led by semiconductor and AI stocks; Intel, ASML, Lam Research, Micron, Applied Materials all show positive momentum. Nvidia joins debt-fueled infrastructure race, raising bubble concerns. AI cost crisis remains un-priced.
sectors.implications: Tech remains the primary driver of market gains, but valuation and concentration risk are elevated. AI infrastructure spending is accelerating, but profitability and sustainability are key questions.

sectors.trend: Oil prices slide on US-Iran ceasefire deal, but storage hubs hit decades-low levels. Europe gas storage gets little relief. Gold falls on hawkish Fed and peace deal jitters. Goldman cuts gold target by $500.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risk premium is easing for oil, but supply constraints remain. Gold is under pressure from higher-for-longer rates. Energy transition and renewables interest rise amid Iran war fallout.

sectors.trend: Santander surpasses Inditex as Spain's most valuable company. Indian banks line up $2.5B debt sales. Canada regulator cuts bank capital to boost lending. UK bonds fall on political risk. Fed's hawkish stance supports dollar.
sectors.implications: Financial sector benefits from higher rates but faces regulatory and political headwinds. Emerging market currencies and bonds are volatile. Dollar strength persists.

sectors.trend: Volkswagen Golf R launched in Malaysia. Toyota CEO questions model proliferation. Carvana pushes into new auto market. Qantas plans world's longest flight. Gas prices fall below $4.
sectors.implications: Auto sector is adapting to EV transition and changing consumer preferences. Lower gas prices may boost consumer spending but weigh on EV demand. Logistics and travel are recovering.

sectors.trend: FDA panel considers first mRNA flu vaccine. Eli Lilly pipeline shows early signals. AI-driven drug discovery continues.
sectors.implications: Healthcare innovation remains robust, with mRNA technology expanding beyond COVID. Regulatory approvals are key catalysts.

key_themes: Hawkish Fed pivot under new Chair Warsh surprises markets
Geopolitical risk: US-Iran ceasefire, Lebanon truce hopes, Hormuz Strait concerns
AI infrastructure boom and bubble risk
Record equity inflows concentrated in tech
Indian IPO wave: NSE and Jio Platforms
UK political risk after Burnham win
Dollar strength and emerging market currency pressure
AI cost crisis un-priced by Wall Street

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into short-duration bonds, value stocks, and commodities. Reduce exposure to high-duration equities.
Maintain energy and defense exposure. Use options for tail-risk hedging. Hold cash for buying opportunities.
Tactical profit-taking on AI leaders. Rotate into AI beneficiaries with reasonable valuations. Monitor Nvidia debt issuance.
Reduce UK gilt exposure. Hedge GBP downside. Favor UK exporters on weak pound.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Emerging market currency and debt stress
risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on EM currencies with strong reserves (INR, SGD). Avoid high-yield EM debt. Monitor Indonesia and Kenya.
Maintain strategic energy holdings. Use oil futures for hedging. Monitor storage levels.
Underweight banks with high legacy IT costs. Favor fintech and AI-native firms. Monitor JPMorgan's AI access restrictions.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy semiconductor equipment stocks on pullbacks (ASML, AMAT, LRCX)
opportunities.rationale: AI and WFE spending cycle is accelerating; near-term dip from Fed noise is a buying opportunity.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Indian IPO exposure (NSE, Jio Platforms) on listing
opportunities.rationale: India's capital market and digital economy growth are structural. Mega IPOs offer rare access to high-quality assets.

opportunities.recommendation: Go long on Uber (UBER) on earnings growth and mobility recovery
opportunities.rationale: Wall Street sees strong earnings growth ahead; lower gas prices and travel demand support.
Brightening prospects for foundry business and chip sector leadership.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in renewable energy and infrastructure
opportunities.rationale: Iran war and geopolitical risk are accelerating renewables interest; EU recycling mandates also support.

defensive_moves.rationale: Fed is hawkish and rates are higher for longer; cash yields 4%+ with no duration risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put spreads on QQQ or SPY
defensive_moves.rationale: Elevated geopolitical risk and Fed uncertainty warrant tail-risk protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-growth tech with no earnings
defensive_moves.rationale: AI cost crisis and rising rates pressure unprofitable names; rotate to profitable AI plays.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Buy gold on dips as geopolitical hedge
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold selloff is overdone; peace deal jitters and Fed hawkishness are temporary. Long-term central bank buying supports.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are in a tug-of-war between Fed hawkishness and AI-driven tech momentum. The next two weeks could be bumpy, but dip-buying is likely to persist. Oil volatility and geopolitical headlines will drive intraday swings. The dollar remains strong, pressuring EM assets. Bitcoin faces downside risk.
market_outlook.long_term: Structural growth in AI, India, and renewable energy remains intact. Fed policy normalization and geopolitical resolution could unlock further upside. However, concentration risk in tech and un-priced AI cost pressures are key risks. Earnings growth will be critical to sustain valuations.

Bitcoin price and options open interest
